name: Gorilla Mux (Go HTTP Router)

performance.notes: Slower than httprouter/chi due to regex matching; adequate for most use cases

agent_readiness.notes: Powerful Go HTTP router with regex route matching. import 'github.com/gorilla/mux'. r := mux.NewRouter(). r.HandleFunc('/products/{id:[0-9]+}', ProductHandler).Methods('GET'). r.HandleFunc('/articles/{category}/{id:[0-9]+}', ArticleHandler). vars := mux.Vars(r)['id']. r.PathPrefix('/static/').Handler(http.StripPrefix('/static/', http.FileServer(http.Dir('./static')))). Subrouters: s := r.PathPrefix('/api').Subrouter(). r.Use(LoggingMiddleware). r.Host('www.example.com'). Named routes: r.Get('article').URL('category', 'tech', 'id', '42'). Schemes: r.Schemes('https'). Headers matching: r.Headers('X-Requested-With', 'XMLHttpRequest'). http.ListenAndServe(':8080', r).

gotchas: Archived in 2022 — in maintenance mode, no new features
Regex matching is slower than chi or httprouter
gorilla project revived by community in 2023 but still in maintenance
Large dependency for what's essentially a router
best_when: When regex route matching is specifically needed
Legacy Go services that predate chi
avoid_when: New projects — use chi or stdlib net/http with Go 1.22+ patterns
summary: Gorilla Mux is a Go HTTP router with regex route matching. r := mux.NewRouter(); r.HandleFunc('/items/{id:[0-9]+}', handler).Methods('GET'). mux.Vars(r) for path vars. Subrouters, middleware, named routes, host/scheme matching. BSD-3-Clause. Archived 2022 (maintenance only). Slower than chi. Best for existing gorilla/mux codebases. New projects should use chi or Go 1.22+ stdlib patterns.
